steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage lists trophic levels in ascending order: base → second → third → blank → fourth (highest). The blank introduces the final, culminating level of a sequence that has been moving upward step by step. The transition must signal "this is the last item in the list."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In fact,': emphasizes or restates a prior point — does not advance a sequential list. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Besides,': adds something incidental or supplementary — not the next ordered step. ✗
  • Choice C — 'For example,': introduces a specific illustration — not the culminating level of a sequence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Finally,': marks the endpoint of an ordered series — exactly what is needed after covering the base, second, and third levels. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
